sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: The Playage Backoffice System is the centralized administrative suite for orchestrating the entire gaming platform. It gives authorized operators a single control surface to configure system settings, monitor live performance, track finances, manage partners, and ensure compliance. Every operational area—user activity, payments, gaming products, CRM, and infrastructure health—feeds into this console so that teams can make quick, well-informed decisions.

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
The Backoffice exists to keep day-to-day operations efficient, transparent, and auditable. Core goals include:
Real-time performance monitoring to guarantee that services stay stable and responsive.
User and partner administration with configurable roles, permissions, and access history.
Financial oversight covering deposits, withdrawals, balance reconciliation, and payouts.
Product management for sportsbook, casino, live games, and third-party integrations.
CRM & promotions to manage campaigns, loyalty programs, promo codes, and notifications.
Analytics & reporting that provide on-demand dashboards for business stakeholders and regulators.

sections.heading: Scope
sections.text:
This documentation set focuses on the main administrative capabilities inside the Playage Backoffice:
Platform monitoring: Track infrastructure health, scheduled tasks, and security alerts in real time.
User & role management: Create, update, or deactivate operator accounts, apply RBAC, and audit session history.
Player operations: View verified documents, enforce responsible-gaming rules, and investigate transactions.
Financial tracking: Reconcile payments, follow revenue KPIs, and export financial data for accounting teams.
Sportsbook & casino operations: Configure markets, odds, game catalogs, and provider-level settings.
CRM & promotions: Launch marketing campaigns, automate bonus eligibility, and deliver targeted communications.
System configuration & maintenance: Tune platform parameters, manage email templates, and schedule upgrades/backups.

sections.heading: Document Objectives
sections.text:
The guide is designed to help team members:
Navigate every module with confidence and understand its business impact.
Execute repetitive operational tasks consistently with step-by-step procedures.
Troubleshoot common scenarios without escalating to engineering.
Maintain compliance, data integrity, and a verifiable audit trail.

sections.heading: Intended Audience
sections.text:
Platform administrators / super admins who own full-tenant configuration.
Operations and support teams who respond to customer requests and monitor service quality.
Finance and compliance officers who reconcile funds and run regulatory reporting.
Marketing and CRM specialists who design campaigns and loyalty journeys.
Technical and integration teams who manage external services, APIs, and maintenance windows.

sections.heading: Summary
sections.text: The Playage Backoffice is the operational core of the Playage Gaming ecosystem. It unifies management of players, partners, products, financials, and compliance signals in one workspace. By following the structure outlined in this documentation, teams can scale their workflows, maintain transparency, and keep the platform healthy for every stakeholder.

sections.heading: Getting Started
sections.text:
The Getting Started section provides essential guidance for new administrators, operators, and partners using the Playage Backoffice — the unified control system for managing all platform operations. This section is designed to introduce the core functionalities, help you set up your account, and guide you through the key interface elements to ensure efficient use from the first session.
By following these instructions, users will gain a clear understanding of the platform's structure, interface, and security best practices.

sections.heading: 2.1.1 Login and Authentication
sections.text:
Access to the Playage Backoffice is restricted to authorized users. For secure and smooth login, each administrator must follow these steps:
Open the Official Backoffice URL in your browser.
Enter Your Registered Email Address and Password .
Click "Sign In" to begin the authentication process.
Two-Factor Authentication (2FA): If enabled, enter the verification code sent to your registered email or generated by your authenticator app (e.g., Google Authenticator).
Once authentication is successful, you will be redirected to the Main Dashboard .
Use only company-approved browsers (Chrome, Edge, Firefox) to access the Backoffice.
Never share your login credentials or verification codes with others.
If you encounter multiple failed login attempts , contact your system administrator to reset your password or verify your account role.
Automatic Session Timeout: Sessions will expire after a period of inactivity to protect your account.
Tip: For added protection, enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) from the Admin Settings → Security Panel .

sections.text:

sections.heading: 2.1.4 Logging Out
sections.text:
To ensure the security of your account and prevent unauthorized access, always log out after completing your administrative tasks.
Click your profile icon in the top-right corner of the dashboard.
Select Logout from the dropdown menu.
Wait for confirmation that your session has been terminated before closing the browser tab.
Always log out before leaving your workstation.
Do not save passwords on shared or public computers.
If you suspect unauthorized activity or have multiple active sessions, use the Force Logout option in Admin → Security Panel .

Navigate the main dashboard and system menus.
Monitor operational data across the platform.
Log out safely and adhere to security best practices.
Mastering these foundational steps will help ensure a smooth and secure experience as you begin managing the Playage Gaming ecosystem.

Switch Between Report Days: Click Today to see only current day's data. Click Yesterday to review previous day's report. Use Last 7 Days or Last 30 Days to compare performance over a period.

sections.heading: 1. Overview
sections.text:
The Player Management & Analytics Dashboard streamlines user account administration, tracks player performance in real-time, and visualizes system health with live analytics.
Core Objectives
Manage player accounts efficiently with simple tools to add, edit, or deactivate users.
Maintain secure access control via role-based permissions.
Monitor key system metrics through real-time charts and tables.
Gain actionable insights from built-in analytics and custom reports.
Ensure compliance with detailed audit logs and security enforcement.

sections.heading: 2.1 Accessing the System
sections.text:
Open the Playage Admin Dashboard URL in a secure browser.
Log in using your administrator credentials.
Complete two-factor authentication (if enabled).
Review the dashboard overview and any active system alerts.

sections.heading: 2.2 Initial Setup
sections.text:
Configure user preferences and notification settings.
Customize the dashboard layout for your workflow.
Enable alerts for key operational or security events.

sections.heading: 3.1 Player Management
sections.text:
Add, update, or deactivate player profiles.
Assign roles, permissions, and access levels.
Track account history, recent logins, and current activity status.

sections.heading: 3.2 Analytics
sections.text:
View real-time metrics on player performance, activity, and retention.
Generate behavioral and financial reports.
Monitor KPIs like conversion rates and account growth over time.

sections.heading: 3.3 Security & Compliance
sections.text:
Review login attempts, access logs, and audit trails.
Enforce password strength and access policy compliance.
Receive alerts on suspicious or unauthorized activity.

sections.heading: 4.1 Monitoring
sections.text:
Use the live dashboard to monitor performance metrics.
Review player sessions, deposits, and engagement trends.
Identify spikes or anomalies through visual analytics.

sections.heading: 4.2 Reporting
sections.text:
Generate detailed reports by player, date range, or metric type.
Export data in PDF, CSV, or HTML formats.
Schedule recurring reports for automated delivery.

sections.heading: 4.3 Maintenance
sections.text:
Perform regular system health checks.
Backup configuration and player data periodically.
Verify third-party integrations to ensure accurate data syncing.

sections.heading: 5. Tips for Effective Use
sections.text:
Regularly review and update player roles and permissions.
Apply dashboard filters to focus on specific timeframes or regions.
Use analytics data to refine engagement and retention strategies.
Keep an eye on real-time alerts and audit logs for compliance.

sections.heading: 6. Support
sections.text:
For technical issues, contact the IT Support Team via the in-app Ticketing or Live Chat module.
Use the Help Section to access FAQs, setup guides, and troubleshooting tips.
Always follow compliance protocols when handling sensitive player information.

sections.heading: Data Loading via Refresh Button
sections.text:
When you first open the Player Management & Analytics Dashboard , the page may display a message:
"No Data Available."
This does not mean that the data is missing — it simply indicates that the information has not yet been fetched from the server.

sections.heading: How to Load the Data
sections.text:
Locate the Refresh Button The Refresh (🔄) icon is visible at the top-right corner of the player list section. Refer to the screenshot above for its position.
Locate the Refresh Button
The Refresh (🔄) icon is visible at the top-right corner of the player list section.
Refer to the screenshot above for its position.
Click on Refresh Clicking this button triggers a live API call to fetch the latest player and analytics data. Depending on system load and data volume, it may take a few seconds to process.
Click on Refresh
Clicking this button triggers a live API call to fetch the latest player and analytics data.
Depending on system load and data volume, it may take a few seconds to process.
Wait for Data to Load Once the data retrieval is complete, the player list and related metrics will automatically appear in the table below. The system ensures real-time synchronization between the server and dashboard view.
Wait for Data to Load
Once the data retrieval is complete, the player list and related metrics will automatically appear in the table below.
The system ensures real-time synchronization between the server and dashboard view.

sections.heading: Why Data Loads After Refresh
sections.text:
To optimize performance , initial page load does not automatically pull large datasets.
The manual refresh approach prevents lag and ensures the dashboard remains responsive.
Each refresh action guarantees up-to-date records are displayed directly from the database.

sections.heading: System Behavior
sections.text:
The API fetches complete data for the selected filters or date range .
If filters are changed (e.g., date, user type, region), clicking Refresh again will reload the corresponding results.
A visual spinner or loading animation may briefly appear while data is being fetched.

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
This mechanism helps Super Admins and Managers:
Efficiently load heavy player datasets without impacting performance.
Control when and how the system retrieves information.
Reduce unnecessary API calls on initial page load.

sections.heading: Player Data Table Overview
sections.text: The Player Management & Analytics Dashboard displays a comprehensive player dataset in a dynamic, horizontally scrollable table. This table provides a 360° view of every player's registration, identification, account, and system interaction details.

sections.heading: Table Interface
sections.text:
The table supports horizontal scrolling to view additional fields beyond the visible area.
Each column header includes a sorting control (▲ / ▼) , allowing users to arrange records in ascending or descending order.
Rows per page and pagination controls appear at the bottom (e.g., 1–15 of 6479 ).
All data loads dynamically through an API when the page is refreshed or filtered.

sections.heading: Key Data Columns
sections.text:
Below are the major field groups included in the Player Data Table:
Player ID – Unique system-generated identifier for each player.
Username – The login name registered by the player.
First Name / Last Name / Middle Name – Player's full name components.
Personal ID – National or system-issued identity reference.
Email – Primary registered email address.
Mobile / Phone – Contact information associated with the account.
Register Date – Date when the account was created.
Verified – Indicates whether the player's KYC verification is complete.
Document Number / Type / Issue Date / Expiration Date / Issued By – Official document verification details.
2FA Verification – Shows whether two-factor authentication is enabled.
QR Code Is Applied – Status of QR-based account identification.
Address / City / State / Zip Code / Country – Player's residence details.
Birth City / Birth Department / Birth Date – Personal birth record details.
Citizenship – Player's country of citizenship.
Gender – Registered gender information.
Title – Optional title prefix (e.g., Mr., Ms., Dr.).
Affiliate ID – Reference to the affiliate partner or campaign source.
BTag / Promo Code – Used for tracking marketing and promotional attribution.
Registration Source – Origin channel (e.g., web, mobile, API).
Last Login Date / Last Wrong Login Date / Wrong Login Attempts – User access logs for monitoring login behavior.
External ID – Unique external mapping identifier (used for integrations).
RF-ID – Linked RFID reference (if enabled in system integration).
Balance – Displays the player's current wallet balance.
Is Email Subscribed – Indicates whether the user opted for email updates.
Subscribed to Newsletter / Push Notifications – Displays marketing communication preferences.
TC Version Acceptance Date – Latest Terms & Conditions acceptance record.
Custom Player Category – Segment or label applied manually or via automation.
Player Category – Default system-assigned classification.
AML Status – Anti-Money Laundering check result.
PEP Status – Politically Exposed Person check status.
Income Source – Player's declared source of income.
Exclusion Type / Self Excluded – Details of any responsible gaming or exclusion settings.
Is Casino Blocked / Is Sport Blocked / Is RMT Blocked – Restrictions applied to specific gaming categories.
Verify By – Admin username who verified the player's identity.
Action – Set of icons to view, edit, or manage a player's record directly.

sections.heading: Functional Highlights
sections.text:
Sortable Columns: Each column can be sorted to organize data by specific fields.
Resizable Columns: Wider columns (like Email , Document Info , Address ) are adjustable for better visibility.
Horizontal Scroll: Use the horizontal scrollbar at the bottom to access all 50+ columns.
Pagination: Supports configurable row limits (e.g., 15, 30, 50 records per page).
Real-time Updates: Data refreshes automatically after API load or manual refresh.

sections.heading: Tip
sections.text:
For optimal viewing:
Use a wide-screen layout or zoom out slightly to view more columns simultaneously.
Use filters or the search bar to narrow down players by ID, Email, or Username.
Combine sorting and filters to isolate specific segments like verified users, self-excluded players, or high-balance accounts.

sections.heading: Wallet Management (Credit / Debit Adjustment)

sections.text:
This tool is designed to:
Handle credit or debit adjustments to a player's account.
Perform manual settlements when automated transactions require administrative intervention.
Support multi-currency operations , ensuring accurate balance management across regions.
Access the Wallet Panel In the Player List table, locate the Action column. Click the Wallet (💳) icon to open the Update Wallet popup.

In the Player List table, locate the Action column.

Credit → Add funds to the player's wallet.
Debit → Deduct funds from the player's wallet.
Enter Transaction Details Amount → Specify the exact value to adjust. Currency → Choose the correct currency type from the dropdown. (Optional) Add remarks or notes for internal tracking.

The system validates the transaction and immediately updates the player's balance.

sections.heading: System Behavior
sections.text:
Each wallet adjustment triggers an audit log entry to maintain compliance and traceability.
Real-time balance updates are reflected in the dashboard upon successful API response.
Unauthorized roles cannot access this feature — it is restricted to Super Admins .

sections.heading: Tip
sections.text:
Use the wallet adjustment function only when:
Automated payment reconciliation fails, or
A legitimate manual correction is needed after verification by the finance team.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: The Player Details & Control Panel allows administrators to view, verify, and manage complete player information from a single interface. It opens when the admin clicks the "Eye (👁️) View" button from the Player List Table .

sections.heading: 1. Access Player Details
sections.text:
Go to the Player List table.
Locate the Action column on the right side.
Click on the 👁️ (View) button next to a player's record.
A new panel will open displaying that player's General Information Page by default.

sections.heading: 2. Navigate the General Tab
sections.text:
The General tab is the first section under the Player Details panel. It includes several categorized sub-sections to easily manage each player's profile.
Available Sub-Sections:
Player Info – Displays basic personal and account information (Name, ID, Contact, KYC status, etc.).
Additional Info – Shows extra player attributes, preferences, or system tags.
Player Privacy Info – Contains privacy and data protection-related settings.
Player Verification – Lists verification documents and KYC progress.
Notes – Allows admin to view or add remarks about player interactions or updates.
Player Documents & Images – Displays uploaded identification and related files.
Emails – Shows player's email communications or system notifications.

sections.heading: 3. Manage Player Data
sections.text:
Each sub-section includes Action Buttons such as: View / Edit / Delete / Verify / Reject / Download
Each sub-section includes Action Buttons such as:
View / Edit / Delete / Verify / Reject / Download
Admins can update records, approve documents, or perform verification actions directly within each sub-tab.
All actions are role-restricted — only authorized users (e.g., Super Admin, Compliance Officer) can make updates.

sections.heading: 4. Real-Time Control
sections.text:
Any changes or actions (like document verification or email updates) are instantly synced with the player database .
The dashboard ensures real-time accuracy across connected modules such as KYC, Reports, and Analytics.

sections.heading: Key Advantages
sections.text:
Centralized control for complete player management.
Easy navigation across all information categories.
Quick access to actions (Verify, Reject, Update) without switching pages.

sections.heading: Finance Tab – User Guide
sections.text:
The Finance Tab in the Player Management & Analytics Dashboard helps administrators manage and review player financial information — including key performance metrics, account balances, and manual payment adjustments.
This tab is divided into three main sections:
Player KPIs
Player Balance
Payment

sections.heading: 1. Player Kpis
sections.text:
Displays financial performance insights of each player.
Shows deposit history, withdrawals, bonus usage, and overall transaction summaries.
Helps identify high-value players or those with unusual spending patterns.
Use this tab to analyze player activity trends and revenue contributions.
Example use case:
A manager can view which players have the highest deposit-to-withdrawal ratio or are most active in a specific date range.

sections.heading: 2. Player Balance
sections.text:
Provides a real-time overview of the player's wallet balance .
Shows the current available balance for each currency the player uses.
Admins can verify if the balance reflects all recent transactions (deposits, bets, or withdrawals).
Useful for cross-checking discrepancies or confirming fund availability before making adjustments.
Example use case:
Before processing a refund, an admin checks this tab to confirm the player's latest balance.

sections.heading: 3. Payment
sections.text:
Allows admins to add correctional payments manually (credit or debit).
Supports multi-currency transactions – choose the correct currency type from the dropdown.
Admins can input the amount, type, and note to document the reason for the adjustment.
Once confirmed, the system updates the player's wallet and logs the change in the audit history.
Example use case:
If a deposit was missed by the payment gateway, the finance team can manually add the amount using the Payment option.

sections.heading: How to Use the Finance Tab
sections.text:
Open a player's detail panel by clicking the eye (👁️) icon in the player list.
Navigate to the Financial tab.
Choose between Player KPIs , Player Balance , or Payment depending on your task.
Use the filters and input fields as needed.
Always click Save or Add Payment to confirm updates.

sections.heading: Tips
sections.text:
Double-check player identity before adjusting payments.
Use notes for every transaction to maintain transparent records.
For auditing, combine Finance Tab data with Reports Tab (explained next).

sections.heading: Reports Tab Overview
sections.text: The Reports section in the Player Management & Analytics Dashboard provides a detailed record of each player's activities, financial history, and interactions within the system. It is designed to help administrators, compliance officers, and finance teams access accurate and categorized data — all in one place.

sections.heading: Primary Objectives
sections.text:
View and analyze player activity logs across multiple modules.
Track financial and betting records in real-time.
Identify transaction patterns and responsible gaming compliance.
Facilitate audit, investigation, and customer support queries efficiently.

sections.heading: Navigating to Reports
sections.text:
Go to Player List → click the 👁 (View) button beside a player's name.
Inside the player's control panel, select the Reports tab from the top navigation.
The Reports tab opens with multiple sub-sections related to the player's data history.

sections.heading: Sub-Tabs Overview
sections.text: Each sub-tab represents a specific data domain. Below is an overview of all available options:

sections.heading: 1. Sportsbook History
sections.text:
Displays all historical sportsbook betting activity.
Shows bet IDs, event names, odds, results, and settlement status.
Useful for tracking winning or losing patterns, and refund claims.

sections.heading: 2. Gaming History
sections.text:
Lists all casino, slots, or other in-game activities by the player.
Includes game name, session ID, start/end time, and win/loss results.
Helps identify engagement trends and session frequency.

sections.heading: 3. Transaction
sections.text:
Contains detailed records of deposits, withdrawals, and manual adjustments.
Each entry includes transaction ID, method (e.g., card, wallet, crypto), status, and timestamp.
Enables finance teams to verify payment accuracy and resolve disputes.

sections.heading: 4. Sports Bets
sections.text:
Summarizes open and settled bets for sports events.
Displays key information such as stake, odds, and payout value.
Supports both live and pre-match bets for auditing and customer service.

sections.heading: 5. Bonuses
sections.text:
Shows all promotional bonuses assigned to or redeemed by the player.
Includes bonus type (e.g., Welcome, Deposit Match), status (Active/Expired), and wagering progress.
Administrators can validate bonus eligibility and usage compliance.

sections.heading: 6. Logins
sections.text:
Logs every player login attempt — including IP address, device, and timestamp.
Indicates successful and failed attempts, aiding in fraud prevention.
Useful for analyzing account access frequency and device usage.

sections.heading: Key Features
sections.text:
Dynamic Data Loading: Each section loads live data from the system in real-time.
Search & Filters: Filter by date, status, transaction type, or event name.
Export Options: Download reports in CSV or XLS format for further analysis.
Audit-Ready: All report data is fully traceable and aligned with compliance requirements.
User-Level Control: Access is restricted based on role permissions (e.g., only Finance or Compliance roles can view financial details).

sections.heading: Best Practices
sections.text:
Always verify date filters before exporting reports to ensure accuracy.
Use Logins and Transaction data together to detect unusual behavior.
Regularly export Bonuses and Gaming History reports for retention and loyalty analysis.

sections.text: The Settings Tab under the Player Management & Analytics Dashboard allows administrators to view and manage a player's configuration options, preferences, and account limitations in one centralized area. Each sub-tab provides specific operational control related to player behavior, compliance, and account customization.

sections.text:
Full Visibility: Every change or request is logged with timestamps and admin identifiers.
Audit-Ready: Supports regulatory compliance by maintaining immutable limit and preference histories.
User-Level Control: Only authorized Compliance or Operations staff can edit or approve limit modifications.
Search & Filters: Search by date, limit type, or action status to locate specific records efficiently.
Export Options: Download complete data in CSV/XLS format for reporting or analysis.

sections.heading: Best Practices
sections.text:
Always verify player consent before modifying social or marketing preferences.
Use the Limit Change History to confirm that all updates comply with responsible gaming standards.
Export Client Limit History regularly for internal audits.

sections.heading: Log History Overview
sections.text: The Log History section provides a detailed chronological record of all player-related actions and system events. It allows administrators to monitor changes, verify activities, and ensure transparency within the platform.

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
The primary goal of the Log History tab is to:
Maintain a complete audit trail for all player-specific operations.
Support compliance and accountability by showing who performed what action and when.
Help admins trace system behavior and resolve issues or disputes efficiently.

sections.heading: How to Access
sections.text:
Navigate to the Player Management & Analytics Dashboard .
Select the Player whose logs you want to review.
Open the Log History tab from the top navigation panel.
The section will load all activity logs associated with that player.

sections.heading: Log Categories
sections.text:
Depending on system configuration, the Log History may include:
Login / Logout Events – Tracks user access times and IP addresses.
Profile Updates – Records edits made to player details (email, KYC, contact info, etc.).
Financial Adjustments – Displays wallet changes, manual credits, or balance corrections.
Betting & Gaming Activity – Lists sports or casino bets placed and their outcomes.
Security Actions – Notes password resets, role changes, or access restrictions.
System Messages – Logs backend API responses or synchronization activities.

sections.heading: Functional Highlights
sections.text:
Search & Filter: Use filters to narrow results by date range, action type, or admin user.
Timestamped Records: Every entry includes a precise date and time for traceability.
Export Options: Logs can be exported in CSV or PDF formats for auditing or reporting.
No Data Message: If "No Data Available" appears, it means no recent actions were logged for the selected player or date range.

sections.heading: Best Practices
sections.text:
Review log history regularly to monitor unusual account activity.
Export and archive monthly logs for compliance and security audits .
Cross-check log entries with Reports or Financial Transactions to ensure accuracy.
Restrict log-access permissions to authorized admins only for data confidentiality.
End of Section
This section helps ensure complete operational transparency within the Playage Admin System by maintaining a verifiable record of all player-level actions.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: The Add Player popup allows administrators to quickly register a new player in the Playage system. It captures all essential player details — such as identification, contact, address, and account information — ensuring the new account is properly verified and ready for activity tracking.

sections.heading: Step-by-Step Guide
sections.text:
Go to the Player List page under the Player Management & Analytics Dashboard .
Click the "+ New" button at the top-right corner of the player table.
A popup titled "Add Player" will appear on the screen.

sections.heading: 2. Filling Out Player Information
sections.text:
The popup form is divided into several sections for clarity:
Username * – Enter a unique username for the player.
Password * – Set a secure login password.
First Name * / Last Name * / Middle Name – Enter full name details.
Gender – Select the player's gender from the dropdown.
Email * – Enter the player's primary email address.
Mobile * – Add the player's phone number.
Language – Choose the preferred communication language.
Address – Enter the complete residential address.
City / Zip Code – Fill in the relevant location details.
Region – Choose the player's region or province.
Currency * – Select the player's operating currency (e.g., TRY, EUR).
Account Status – Choose the default account status (e.g., Open).
Passport ID – Enter a valid passport or ID number if applicable.
Is Resident – Tick if the player resides in the primary jurisdiction.
Subscribed to Email – Mark if the player agrees to receive newsletters or marketing emails.

sections.heading: 3. Submitting the Form
sections.text:
Review all entered details for accuracy.
Click Submit to finalize registration.
The system will: Validate all mandatory fields. Create a new player record in the database. Display a success confirmation once saved.
The system will:
Validate all mandatory fields.
Create a new player record in the database.
Display a success confirmation once saved.
If any validation fails (e.g., duplicate username or missing required field), the system highlights the issue for correction.

sections.heading: System Behavior
sections.text:
A new player record instantly appears in the Player List Table once added.
All related modules (Analytics, Wallet, Reports) automatically sync the new profile.
Every "Add Player" action is logged in the Audit Trail for compliance tracking.

sections.heading: Tips
sections.text:
Always double-check that username and email are unique before submitting.
Use strong passwords (minimum 8 characters, including symbols and numbers).
Ensure currency and region match the player's operational market.
For mass registrations, use the Bulk Import feature under the Reports or Tools menu.

sections.heading: Example Use Case
sections.text: A customer support officer needs to onboard a new player manually who registered via an offline campaign. They click "+ New", fill in all mandatory fields, verify the details, and submit — instantly adding the player to the system.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text:

Registered Count – Total number of players who completed registration.
Deposited Users Count – Number of players who made at least one deposit.
Deposit Count / Amount – Total deposits and their aggregated values in various currencies (EUR, USD, TRY, etc.).
Average Deposit Amount – The mean value of deposits per player, helping to identify high-value segments.
These metrics can be filtered and exported for performance reviews or strategic marketing analysis.

sections.heading: Interface Reference

sections.heading: 1. Executive Overview
sections.text:
The Self-Exclusion Portal is a regulatory-compliance tool that provides full visibility into players under self-restriction . It assists the Responsible Gaming Team in enforcing exclusion policies, tracking reactivation timelines, and generating compliance reports.
Core Objectives
Enforce responsible-gaming protocols and self-exclusion regulations.
Provide real-time visibility into excluded and reinstated accounts.
Streamline communication between compliance and support teams.
Maintain complete audit trails for legal and regulatory review.
Automate alerts for upcoming expiration or policy violations.

sections.heading: 2. Key Features
sections.text:
Centralized Exclusion Register: Displays all self-excluded clients with reason, start date, and duration.
Automated Status Control: Automatically suspends gameplay, deposits, and withdrawals for excluded users.
Advanced Search Filters: Search by username, email, region, or exclusion type (temporary / permanent).
Compliance Alerts: Receive system notifications before exclusion expiry or rule breach.
Case Notes & Remarks: Add internal comments for compliance review or support reference.
Audit Trails: Tracks who created, modified, or reinstated an exclusion.
Data Export: Export exclusion logs in CSV, PDF, or Excel format for regulatory submission.
Integration Support: Syncs with CRM, fraud-detection, and payment verification modules.

sections.heading: 3.1 Getting Started
sections.text:
Log in to the Playage Admin Portal with compliance or responsible-gaming credentials.
Navigate to Player → Self-Excluded Client Portal .
The system displays all active and archived self-excluded players.
Use the left-panel filters to select exclusion type, region, or date range.

sections.heading: 3.2 Registering a New Self-Exclusion
sections.text:
Click "Add New Exclusion" .
Enter player details (Player ID / Email / Username).
Choose exclusion type (temporary or permanent) and duration.
Provide a justification or player request reference ID.
Click Confirm and Save to activate restrictions immediately.

sections.heading: 3.3 Daily Operations
sections.text:
Review the Active Exclusions Table for current cases.
Approve or deny pending player self-exclusion requests.
Use the "Expiry Alerts" widget to monitor approaching end dates.
Generate daily and monthly reports for compliance audits.

sections.heading: 3.4 Reactivation & Compliance Review
sections.text:
After expiry, the system flags accounts for review instead of auto-activation.
Compliance officers can manually reactivate players after eligibility checks.
All reactivations require dual authorization (Compliance + Admin Team).
The system records timestamp and user ID for every reactivation event.

sections.heading: 4.1 Basic Settings
sections.text:
Role Management: Define who can view / edit self-exclusion records.
Notification Setup: Enable email or in-app alerts for new requests and expirations.
Default Exclusion Periods: Pre-define standard durations (7 days, 30 days, 6 months, permanent).
Dashboard Layout: Customize columns shown in the client list table.

sections.heading: 4.2 Advanced Configuration
sections.text:
Integration with KYC / AML: Automatically cross-verify player identity during exclusion.
API Synchronization: Push exclusion data to partner platforms and third-party compliance networks.
Audit Automation: Enable scheduled export of self-exclusion logs to regulatory repositories.
Performance Optimization: Manage cache refresh for large datasets to ensure dashboard speed.

sections.heading: 6. Security & Compliance Features
sections.text:
Access Control: Only authorized compliance users can manage records.
Data Encryption: Sensitive player data is encrypted in storage and transit.
Audit Logging: Every change is timestamped with user credentials.
GDPR & Responsible-Gaming Compliance: Fully aligns with industry regulations for self-exclusion handling.
Incident Monitoring: Automatic alerts for attempted access by excluded players.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.text:
The User Role Permission Management Console allows administrators to define, edit, and manage user access levels across the Playage admin portal. It ensures that each user has the correct permissions for their operational scope—maintaining security, control, and organizational hierarchy.
This section is particularly important when creating partner users. Before assigning a partner user, the corresponding user role must first be created. The roles available for assignment are configured and managed through this console.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Below is a reference screenshot for understanding the interface layout of the User Role Permission Management Console .
Key Sections:
Roles and Permissions Panel : This is where all roles defined in the system are listed, such as 'Risk', 'Master', 'Test', and more. The roles can be edited, deleted, or new roles can be created using the Add Role button.
Users : Shows the number of users associated with each role. You can click on each role to see the list of users.
Action Buttons : The Edit and Delete icons allow administrators to manage roles.
Add Role Button : This red button at the top right allows the creation of new roles.

sections.heading: 1. Purpose of the Console
sections.text:
The User Role Permission Management Console is used to:
Create new user roles : Define roles for administrators, partners, and other operational staff.
Edit or remove existing roles : Adjust permissions for existing roles as needed.
Control access levels : Assign modules or features to specific roles.
Assign users : Assign users to specific roles and control their access to different parts of the admin portal.
Track updates : Monitor the latest updates or modifications made to access settings.

sections.heading: 2. On-Screen Elements
sections.text: Here's a breakdown of the key on-screen elements available in the User Role Permission Management Console :

sections.heading: 3. Creating a New Role
sections.text:
To create a new role, follow these steps:
Step 1 : Click on the Add Role button in the top-right corner.
Step 2 : Enter a Role Name (e.g., "Marketing Manager" or "Partner Reporting Viewer").
Step 3 : Assign permissions for different modules (e.g., Dashboard, Reports, User Management, etc.).
Step 4 : Click Save to create the new role. The role will now appear in the list with 0 users until assigned.

sections.heading: 4. Editing an Existing Role
sections.text:
To edit an existing role:
Step 1 : Locate the role you wish to modify.
Step 2 : Click the Edit (🖉) icon next to the role.
Step 3 : Adjust the role's permissions by toggling on or off the relevant features or modules.
Step 4 : Click Save to apply the changes. The changes will be reflected under the Last Update column.

sections.heading: 5. Deleting a Role
sections.text:
To delete a role, ensure that the role is not assigned to any users. Once confirmed, follow these steps:
Step 1 : Click the Delete (🗑) icon next to the role you wish to delete.
Step 2 : Confirm the action. The role will be permanently removed from the system.

sections.heading: 6. Assigning Users to a Role
sections.text:
To assign users to a role:
Step 1 : Navigate to the User List page.
Step 2 : Find the user account you wish to modify and click on it to edit.
Step 3 : In the user profile, select the appropriate role from the dropdown menu.
Step 4 : Save the changes. The user is now assigned to the selected role. The role's user count will automatically update in the User Role Permission Management Console .

sections.heading: 7. Use Case Example
sections.text:
Example 1: Creating a Role for Partners An Operations Admin needs to create a new role titled "Partner Reporting Viewer" for partner users. This role should have read-only access to the reports section but no access to sensitive settings.
Click the Add Role button.
Enter "Partner Reporting Viewer" as the role name.
Assign read-only access to reports and analytics modules.
Save the role.
Assign the role to specific partner users.
This process ensures that partners can only view reports without the ability to make changes or access sensitive features.

sections.heading: 8. Administrative Notes
sections.text:
Super Admins or Master Roles can modify or delete any role.
All changes are timestamped under the Last Update column.
Role definitions take effect immediately across all users assigned to the role.
Periodically review roles to ensure compliance with security and organizational standards.

sections.heading: 9. Security Recommendations
sections.text:
Implement role-based segregation of duties to avoid overlapping privileges.
Follow the minimal-access principle : grant users only the permissions necessary for their role.
Regularly review and remove inactive roles to maintain a clean and efficient user management system.
This concludes the guide for the User Role Permission Management Console . You can now begin creating, editing, and managing user roles to ensure efficient access control and compliance within the Playage admin platform.

sections.heading: 1. Introduction
sections.text: The User Role Permission Management Console is a powerful feature that allows administrators to define, edit, and manage user roles and their associated permissions within the Playage Admin Console . This tool ensures that users are granted access only to the resources they need, maintaining security and control within the platform. Roles can be customized, and permissions can be dynamically applied to users based on their roles, ensuring an organized and secure access control system.

sections.heading: 2. Purpose
sections.text:
The purpose of the User Role Permission Management Console is to:
Provide a centralized view of all roles configured in the system.
Allow administrators to assign, remove, and manage permissions for specific roles.
Maintain security by ensuring that each user has the appropriate level of access based on their role.
Enable auditing and control by tracking changes to roles and permissions.

sections.text:

Key Elements:

Given : This section shows the permissions already assigned to the selected role.
Search Bar : Allows for filtering and searching for roles and permissions dynamically.

Role Management : Admins can create new roles, edit existing roles, and assign them to users.
Permission Assignment : Admins can assign or remove permissions for each role, allowing them to control access to various parts of the system.
Dynamic Role Updates : Changes made to roles and permissions are applied dynamically and immediately reflect on the assigned users.
Audit Tracking : Every change to roles and permissions is logged and timestamped for audit purposes.

sections.heading: How to Add a New Bonus – Step-by-Step Guide
sections.text: The Bonus Management Dashboard allows administrators to create and configure different types of bonuses for players. This section explains how to add a new bonus, set its configuration, and save it for activation.

sections.heading: 1. Access the Bonus Management Dashboard
sections.text:
From the main menu , go to System Configuration → Bonus Management Dashboard .
The dashboard displays all existing bonuses and provides tools to add, edit, or manage them.

sections.heading: 2. Select Bonus Type
sections.text:
Click on the Bonus Type dropdown located near the top of the dashboard.
Choose the desired type of bonus you want to create, such as: Deposit Bonus Free Bet Bonus Cashback Bonus Free Spins Custom Bonus
Choose the desired type of bonus you want to create, such as:
Deposit Bonus
Free Bet Bonus
Cashback Bonus
Free Spins
Custom Bonus
Each bonus type determines the available configuration options in the next step.

sections.heading: 3. Click on "Add Bonus"
sections.text:
After selecting the desired bonus type, click the "Add" or "+ New Bonus" button.
This opens the Bonus Creation Popup where you can enter all required bonus details.

sections.heading: 4. Fill in Bonus Details
sections.text:
In the Bonus Creation Popup , fill in all mandatory fields to configure the new bonus:
Name: Enter the bonus name (e.g., Welcome Free Bet ).
Region: Choose the region or target market for the bonus.
Status: Select whether the bonus should be Active or Inactive .
Bonus Program Period: Define the start and end dates of the bonus campaign.
Expiration Type & Date: Choose how long the bonus remains valid.
Currency: Select the applicable currency.
Is Trial Bonus: Check this option if it's meant for testing.
Description: Provide a short description of the bonus (e.g., "Free bet for new users on registration").
🔹 You can also configure Game Type , Reward Conditions , and other advanced options depending on the selected bonus type.

sections.heading: 5. Save the Bonus
sections.text:
Once all the details are filled in, click the "Save" button at the bottom of the popup.
The new bonus will now appear in the Bonus List on the dashboard.

sections.heading: 6. Verify Bonus Creation
sections.text:
After saving, confirm that the newly added bonus is visible in the list with the correct status.
If necessary, you can later edit , activate , or deactivate it directly from the dashboard.

sections.heading: Tips
sections.text:
Always double-check the bonus validity period and region selection before saving.
Keep the status as "Inactive" if the bonus needs to be reviewed before activation.
You can create multiple bonuses of different types to support various promotional strategies.

sections.heading: View Bonus Statistics and Export Reports
sections.text: The Bonus Management Dashboard allows administrators to analyze detailed performance metrics of each bonus program through the Statistics feature. This section explains how to view bonus data and export reports for further analysis.

sections.heading: 1. Access the Bonus Management Dashboard
sections.text:
From the main menu , go to System Configuration → Bonus Management Dashboard .
The dashboard will display a list of existing bonuses in the Bonus List table.

sections.heading: 2. Locate the Bonus Entry
sections.text:
Scroll through the list to find the bonus you wish to review.
Each row includes essential details such as Type , Product , Start Date , End Date , Status , and Action Buttons on the far right.

sections.heading: 3. Click the "Statistics" Button
sections.text:
Under the Action column, click on the 📈 Statistics button next to the desired bonus.
This action will open the Bonus Statistics Popup , displaying comprehensive details about the selected bonus.
The popup provides two main sections of information:

sections.heading: A. Information Overview
sections.text:
Bonus Program Period: Displays the start and end date of the bonus.
Expiration Details: Shows the expiration days and bonus money expiration date.
Bonus Type & Product: Indicates the type of bonus (e.g., Free Spin, Cashback) and the associated product.
Number of Players Awarded: Displays the total count of players who received the bonus.
Bonus Status: Shows whether the bonus is active, expired, or deactivated.

sections.heading: B. Bonus Report Section
sections.text:
This section provides a tabular view of all player-level data related to the bonus, including:
Player ID and Username
Type of Bonus Received
Initial Bonus Amount
Remaining Balance
Status (e.g., Active, Redeemed, Expired)

sections.heading: 4. Export Bonus Report
sections.text:
At the top-right corner of the Statistics Popup , you will find the Export button.
Click the dropdown arrow beside it to choose the desired export format: CSV – for spreadsheet-compatible data files. XLS – for Microsoft Excel reports.
Click the dropdown arrow beside it to choose the desired export format:
CSV – for spreadsheet-compatible data files.
XLS – for Microsoft Excel reports.
The export feature becomes available only when the bonus report contains data.
Once exported, the report can be used for financial tracking, audit documentation, or performance analysis.

sections.text:
Use the Statistics view frequently to monitor player engagement and redemption rates.
Export reports regularly for cross-verification with financial or CRM data.

sections.heading: Edit Existing Bonus – Step-by-Step Guide
sections.text: The Bonus Management Dashboard allows administrators to modify any previously created bonus using the Edit option. This function ensures flexibility in updating bonus configurations, dates, or other details as business requirements evolve.

sections.heading: Steps to Edit a Bonus
sections.text:
1. Access the Bonus Management Dashboard
From the main menu , navigate to System Configuration → Bonus Management Dashboard .
The dashboard displays a table listing all existing bonuses with their details and status.
2. Locate the Bonus to Edit
Scroll through the Bonus List to find the entry you wish to modify.
Each row contains key fields such as Bonus Type , Product , Start/End Date , Status , and an Action column.
3. Click the "Edit" Button
In the Action column (last column of the table), click the ✏️ Edit button corresponding to the desired bonus.
This opens the Edit Bonus Popup with all the existing details of that bonus.
4. Update Bonus Details Inside the popup, you can modify:
Bonus Name – Adjust the name if required.
Regions – Add or remove target markets.
Status – Change from Inactive to Active , or vice versa.
Bonus Program Period – Extend or shorten the campaign duration.
Expiration Type and Date – Modify bonus validity.
Currency or Product Type – Update applicable options.
Description – Edit the internal or external description for clarity.
💡 Optional: You can also modify financial factors , game conditions , or eligibility rules depending on the bonus configuration type.
5. Save the Changes
After making the required edits, click the Save button at the bottom of the popup.
The system will update the bonus instantly and reflect the new details in the Bonus List table.

sections.heading: Key Notes
sections.text:
Edited bonuses are tracked in the system's audit logs for compliance.
Always verify that date and status fields are correct before saving.
If a bonus is already active, changes may require admin-level approval depending on system permissions.

sections.heading: Tip
sections.text:
Use the Edit function for:
Correcting configuration errors
Extending promotional validity
Updating region or eligibility rules
Reactivating previously deactivated bonuses

sections.heading: Executive Overview
sections.text: The Admin Profile Management Dashboard serves as the central hub for managing administrator profiles, user access, and security configurations. It ensures that all administrative identities are securely managed, accurately maintained, and fully compliant with organizational policies.

sections.heading: Core Objectives
sections.text:
Simplify user administration and access control
Strengthen platform security through authentication and monitoring
Improve operational efficiency and governance in user identity management

sections.heading: Profile Management
sections.text:
Edit essential user details (name, email, phone, and password)
Maintain synchronized and accurate profile information across systems
Track change history for auditing and compliance

sections.heading: Access Control
sections.text:
Assign and modify role-based permissions (Admin, Operator, Manager, etc.)
Enforce least-privilege principles by restricting sensitive areas
Protect critical modules from unauthorized access

sections.heading: Identity & Authentication
sections.text:
Support Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) and Single Sign-On (SSO)
Enforce password complexity and rotation policies
Manage active sessions and remotely terminate risky logins

sections.heading: Account Settings
sections.text:
Configure language, time zone, and notification preferences
Manage login and device sessions
Monitor and audit unusual account activities

sections.heading: 1️⃣ Profile Editing
sections.text:
Navigate to User Management → Edit Profile
Update personal fields (Name, Email, Phone, Password)
Save changes — updates are logged automatically

sections.heading: 2️⃣ Role Assignment
sections.text:
Go to Access Control → Role Settings
Select the user and assign a predefined role
Save changes — permissions are applied instantly

sections.heading: 3️⃣ Password & Security Management
sections.text:
Reset forgotten passwords and enforce periodic changes
Enable MFA for additional protection
Review password change and login activity history

sections.heading: 4️⃣ Device Management
sections.text:
View all active devices and login sessions
Revoke access or log out specific sessions remotely

sections.heading: Getting Started
sections.text:
Log in using admin credentials
Access Profile Management from the dashboard menu

sections.heading: Initial Configuration
sections.text:
Update personal details and preferences
Set notification and alert rules
Review connected devices and current sessions

sections.heading: Daily Operations
sections.text:
Monitor active users and login attempts
Review profile updates and audit logs
Generate reports for access and security compliance

sections.heading: Maintenance Tasks
sections.text:
Perform password and access audits
Update inactive accounts and enforce security patches
Backup profile and configuration data regularly

sections.heading: Basic Settings
sections.text:
User Management: Add, edit, or remove users
Role Permissions: Assign privileges and manage hierarchy
System Preferences: Define UI language and personal defaults

sections.heading: Advanced Settings
sections.text:

Custom Workflows: Automate onboarding and approval flows

sections.heading: 1. Access or Login Problems
sections.text:
✅ Verify URL and credentials
✅ Clear browser cache and ensure compatibility
🔧 Fix: Reset password or contact admin support

sections.heading: 2. Feature or Functionality Issues
sections.text:

✅ Confirm no active maintenance is in progress
🔧 Fix: Retry or contact support with logs/screenshots

sections.heading: 3. Performance & Display Issues

✅ Disable browser extensions
🔧 Fix: Refresh or use an alternate browser

Contact IT/DevOps for backend review and apply relevant patches

sections.heading: Summary
sections.text: The Admin Profile Management Dashboard empowers administrators to maintain secure, efficient, and compliant user management operations. It consolidates identity governance, access control, and profile administration into one intuitive interface — essential for operational integrity and data security across the Playage enterprise ecosystem.

sections.heading: How to Enable Google Authenticator (TOTP)
sections.text:
Click Enable Now next to Google Authenticator.
A QR code and backup key appear on the screen.
Open Google Authenticator or a similar TOTP app.
Scan the QR code or manually enter the backup key.
Enter the 6-digit verification code from your app.
On success, 2FA status changes to Enabled .
Save the backup key securely — this is required if you lose access to your device.
Notes:
If the QR code doesn't appear, refresh and ensure pop-ups are allowed.
TOTP codes are time-based — make sure your phone's clock is synchronized.

sections.heading: How to Enable Email Authenticator
sections.text:
Click Enable Now beside Email Authenticator.
A verification code is sent to your registered email.
Enter the code and click Verify / Confirm .
Email 2FA is then activated for future logins.
Notes:
Verify your email is current and can receive messages from @playagegaming.tech .
Email codes may take up to 1–2 minutes to arrive.

sections.heading: Disable / Remove 2FA
sections.text:
When 2FA is active, a Disable / Remove option appears.
You must authenticate using your 2FA code before disabling.
Always log and authorize any 2FA deactivation for security compliance.

sections.heading: Recovery & Backup
sections.text:
Backup Key: Store safely in a password manager or secure location.
Account Recovery: If you lose both TOTP and email access, contact support for identity verification and 2FA reset.
Best Practice: Create a secondary admin account with 2FA for emergency recovery.

sections.heading: Security Best Practices
sections.text:
Prefer Google Authenticator over email for stronger protection.
Store backup keys using a password manager .
Enforce 2FA for all admin and privileged roles .
Conduct regular 2FA policy reviews across accounts.
